segments.text: I made a covenant with my eyes; how then should I look lustfully at a young woman?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For what is the portion from God above, and the heritage from the Almighty on high?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Is it not calamity to the unrighteous, and disaster to the workers of iniquity?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Doesn’t he see my ways, and count all my steps?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: “If I have walked with falsehood, and my foot has hurried to deceit
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: (let me be weighed in an even balance, that God may know my integrity);
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
if my step has turned out of the way, if my heart walked after my eyes, 
if any defilement has stuck to my hands,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: then let me sow, and let another eat. Yes, let the produce of my field be rooted out.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: “If my heart has been enticed to a woman, and I have laid wait at my neighbor’s door,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: then let my wife grind for another, and let others sleep with her.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For that would be a heinous crime. Yes, it would be an iniquity to be punished by the judges,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: for it is a fire that consumes to destruction, and would root out all my increase.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
“If I have despised the cause of my male servantor of my female servant, 
when they contended with me,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: what then will I do when God rises up? When he visits, what will I answer him?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Didn’t he who made me in the womb make him? Didn’t one fashion us in the womb?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: “If I have withheld the poor from their desire, or have caused the eyes of the widow to fail,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: or have eaten my morsel alone, and the fatherless has not eaten of it
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: (no, from my youth he grew up with me as with a father, I have guided her from my mother’s womb);
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if I have seen any perish for want of clothing, or that the needy had no covering;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if his heart hasn’t blessed me, if he hasn’t been warmed with my sheep’s fleece;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if I have lifted up my hand against the fatherless, because I saw my help in the gate;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: then let my shoulder fall from the shoulder blade, and my arm be broken from the bone.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For calamity from God is a terror to me. Because of his majesty, I can do nothing.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: “If I have made gold my hope, and have said to the fine gold, ‘You are my confidence;’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I have rejoiced because my wealth was great, and because my hand had gotten much;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if I have seen the sun when it shined, or the moon moving in splendor,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: and my heart has been secretly enticed, and my hand threw a kiss from my mouth;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: this also would be an iniquity to be punished by the judges, for I would have denied the God who is above.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: “If I have rejoiced at the destruction of him who hated me, or lifted up myself when evil found him
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: (I have certainly not allowed my mouth to sinby asking his life with a curse);
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if the men of my tent have not said,‘Who can find one who has not been filled with his meat?’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: (the foreigner has not camped in the street, but I have opened my doors to the traveler);
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if like Adam I have covered my transgressions, by hiding my iniquity in my heart,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
because I feared the great multitude, and the contempt of families terrified me, 
so that I kept silence, and didn’t go out of the door—
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
oh that I had one to hear me! Behold, here is my signature! Let the Almighty answer me! 
Let the accuser write my indictment!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Surely I would carry it on my shoulder, and I would bind it to me as a crown.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I would declare to him the number of my steps. I would go near to him like a prince.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If my land cries out against me, and its furrows weep together;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if I have eaten its fruits without money, or have caused its owners to lose their life,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: let briers grow instead of wheat, and stinkweed instead of barley.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The words of Job are ended.
